behaviors.description: The speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels so that the summary can attribute statements to specific attendees.
behaviors.justification: The failure can come from real diarization behavior of the speech-to-text model (missing/unstable speaker labels) plus downstream use of those labels. A mocked STT response can hide this by always returning perfect labels. Because correctness depends on a real AI/ML transcription+diarization output that may vary by run/audio, Agentic is the minimum reliable boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Run transcription on a real multi-speaker meeting audio fixture through the production STT provider (diarization enabled), then assert the produced transcript contains speaker labels across turns and that labels are preserved in the object passed to summarization.

behaviors.description: The structured summary contains at least one action item for every attendee who was assigned a task during the meeting.
behaviors.justification: This is a content-quality requirement on extraction/attribution from language, not just schema wiring. Mocking the LLM would hide real misses in assignment detection and attendee mapping. Correctness depends on real model output variability, so Agentic is required.
behaviors.test_description: Use a real transcript fixture with explicit task assignments to multiple attendees, call the production summarization model, parse structured output, and assert every attendee with an assigned task in ground truth has at least one corresponding action item.

behaviors.description: Key decisions mentioned in the transcript appear in the "decisions" section of the summary, not buried in the narrative.
behaviors.justification: This checks semantic extraction and section placement quality from model output. A mock or deterministic stub would not expose real model misclassification/format drift. Since success depends on real model behavior, Agentic is the minimum.
behaviors.test_description: Provide a transcript fixture containing clearly stated decisions, run real summarization, and assert those decisions are present in the structured `decisions` field (and not only in freeform narrative text).

behaviors.description: When the audio file is longer than 60 minutes, the service splits it into segments and processes them sequentially, merging the results into a single coherent summary.
behaviors.justification: The behavior spans multiple sequential segment passes and a merge step; real failures often emerge only across chained AI calls (context fragmentation, cross-segment inconsistency, merge degradation). Single-step agentic or integration tests can miss these sequence effects. Minimum boundary is a multi-step agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Run the long-audio path on a real >60-minute meeting recording through production segmentation/orchestration: verify segment boundaries and sequential processing order, then validate merged output is one structured summary with consistent attendees/action items/decisions across segments.

behaviors.description: The full pipeline — upload audio, transcribe, summarize, deliver structured output — completes without manual intervention for a real meeting recording.
behaviors.justification: This is explicitly end-to-end across upload, external STT, LLM summarization, and delivery, including multiple agentic steps and system boundaries. Lower-level tests cannot catch orchestration and handoff failures across the full chain.
behaviors.test_description: Execute an end-to-end run in a production-like environment using a real meeting recording and real providers: upload file, wait for transcription and summarization completion, then assert final structured payload is delivered to the configured output sink without retries requiring human intervention.
